outcomes.name: Adopt GitHub Enterprise Cloud
outcomes.tagline: Stand up the operating model for GitHub as the enterprise developer platform.
outcomes.description: Move teams from basic GitHub usage to an enterprise-ready foundation for planning, collaboration, governance, automation, and AI-assisted delivery.
success_metrics: Pilot teams can plan, code, review, and automate delivery in GitHub without leaving the platform.
Org-level governance, access, audit, and cost controls are configured and explainable.
Coaches can map completed challenges to an adoption plan for additional teams.

outcomes.name: Migrate projects to GitHub
outcomes.tagline: Plan and execute a safe move from Azure DevOps, Bitbucket, GitLab, or other platforms.
outcomes.description: Use the GitHub platform modules to model a migration path: repository setup, team permissions, planning artifacts, branch protections, CI conversion, automation, and post-migration validation.
success_metrics: A pilot repository has a GitHub-native planning, review, security, and CI/CD path.
Legacy platform controls have a documented GitHub equivalent or migration decision.
The migration lead can explain validation, rollback, and post-cutover ownership.

outcomes.name: Adopt GitHub Advanced Security
outcomes.tagline: Turn security tooling into a repeatable secure-development operating model.
outcomes.description: Enable GHAS, explore real vulnerabilities, remediate findings, protect secrets and dependencies, and run security campaigns that scale across teams.
success_metrics: Teams can identify, prioritize, and remediate CodeQL, secret, and dependency findings.
Security campaigns have owners, scope, evidence, and measurable remediation outcomes.
Developers understand how GHAS changes their daily pull-request workflow.

outcomes.name: Adopt agentic workflows
outcomes.tagline: Move from one-off automation to reviewable agent-driven engineering flows.
outcomes.description: Build safe GitHub-native agents for issue triage, PR review, CI diagnosis, documentation, testing, security, and continuous intelligence.
success_metrics: Each agent has clear triggers, allowed tools, safe outputs, and review points.
Teams can choose candidate workflows based on measurable time saved or risk reduced.
Production patterns are reusable across real repositories with guardrails.

outcomes.name: Adopt Azure SRE Agent
outcomes.tagline: Turn Azure operational signals into evidence-backed investigation and governed remediation.
outcomes.description: Use Azure SRE Agent with Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, Application Insights, service knowledge, source-code context, and GitHub follow-up to reduce operational toil and improve incident response.
success_metrics: An Azure SRE Agent can explain the monitored application, connected Azure resources, and available runbooks.
A controlled incident produces an alert, investigation evidence, likely cause, and recovery action.
Source-code context or a fallback evidence packet connects operational symptoms to governed engineering follow-up.
